findings.message: Destructive action (DELETE) is performed immediately on click with no confirmation step. A misclick removes the item irreversibly with no undo.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation step before deleting (e.g. window.confirm, or a confirm dialog/two-step button), or provide an undo affordance after deletion.

findings.message: No feedback while the DELETE request is in flight: the button is not disabled and there is no pending/loading indicator, so users can double-click and get no sense the action is progressing.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state (e.g. const [pending, setPending] = useState(false)) and disable the button while the request is in flight, showing a spinner or 'Deleting…' label.

findings.message: The fetch result is not checked and there is no error branch: a failed DELETE (network error or non-2xx response) is silently swallowed, then window.location.reload() runs anyway, leaving the user with no indication anything went wrong.
findings.suggestion: Await the response, check response.ok / catch errors, and surface a visible error message (e.g. role="alert") on failure instead of unconditionally reloading.

findings.message: window.location.reload() is used to refresh the list after delete — a full-page reload is a jarring affordance that loses scroll position and any client state, and gives no success confirmation.
findings.suggestion: Update the items list in state (e.g. lift state up and remove the deleted item) and optionally show a brief success toast, instead of a full page reload.
